mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on security and scalability. It simplifies deployment complexity and operational overhead by providing a standardized framework for agent communication. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and potential learning curve for new users.

steps.title: Add the Kagenti Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Kagenti Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Kagenti easily.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Kagenti repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Kagenti CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Kagenti to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/crds/kagenti.io_kagent_crd.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install Kagenti using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti application in the specified namespace, ensuring all necessary components are deployed.

```bash

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --create-namespace --version 0.5.1

```

> Installs Kagenti in the 'kagenti' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for Kagenti, allowing you to deploy an AI agent.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: KAgent
metadata:
  name: example-agent
spec:
  image: kagenti/agent:latest
EOF

```

> Creates an example agent CR.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: KAgent
metadata:
  name: example-agent
spec:
  image: kagenti/agent:latest
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the deployed Custom Resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get kagents -n kagenti

```

> Lists all KAgents in the 'kagenti' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti installation and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data related to the agents deployed.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> Uninstalls the Kagenti Helm release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step will upgrade Kagenti to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get kagents -n kagenti -o yaml > kagents-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --version 0.5.1

kubectl get kagents -n kagenti

```

> Backs up existing KAgent configurations.
> Upgrades Kagenti to the specified version.
> Verifies that the KAgents are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get kagents -n kagenti -o yaml > kagents-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'KAgent not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified KAgent resource does not exist in the namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure the KAgent resource is created and check the namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image for the KAgent is not available or incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in the KAgent configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm release for Kagenti has not been installed or has been deleted.

**Fix:**
Reinstall Kagenti using the Helm install command.

troubleshooting.title: KAgent is not running
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KAgent pod may have crashed or failed to start.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kagenti' for more details.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new agent types and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KAgent listed with 'kubectl get kagents -n kagenti' showing a status of 'Running'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: KAgent
metadata:
  name: example-agent
spec:
  image: kagenti/agent:latest
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
